preamble: The shift from 'dz' to 'j' (dʒ) is the voiced counterpart of 'ts' to 'ch' palatalization, involving the movement of a voiced alveolar affricate to a post-alveolar position.

examples.note: Development of Slavic affricates before high vowels.
Development of Romance affricates before high vowels.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
